task_completion.description: GIMP is open with the target image loaded.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GIMP application window is visible, displaying a real photograph (typically a portrait or headshot) on the main canvas.

task_completion.description: The Filters menu is accessed to find the Distorts category.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Filters' dropdown menu is open, with the 'Distorts' submenu expanded or highlighted.

task_completion.description: The Wind filter dialog box is opened.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog box titled 'Wind' is visible on the screen, containing settings for Style, Direction, and Edge Affected.

task_completion.description: The Wind filter preview shows the directional motion streaks.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The small preview window inside the Wind filter dialog shows the image with thin lines stretching from the edges of the subject, indicating the effect is active.

task_completion.description: The wind effect is successfully applied to the main image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main GIMP canvas displays the final image with clear directional motion streaks (thin white/black lines stretching horizontally from edges) applied to the subject.

integrity.description: The agent used the GIMP graphical user interface to apply the effect.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with GIMP menus and dialogs, rather than terminal windows executing image processing commands like ImageMagick.

integrity.description: The effect was applied to the original provided image.
integrity.visual_evidence: The image in GIMP matches a standard photograph (portrait/headshot) and is not a pre-processed image or a completely different file.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a Python script or terminal to bypass the GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows or text editors with Python/Bash scripts applying Sobel filters or wind effects are visible.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target image is a real photo from Unsplash (typically 687x1031 or 687x458). The GIMP Wind filter is located at Filters → Distorts → Wind. When applied, it creates directional motion streaks by detecting edges and stretching thin white/black lines from them. Look for these asymmetric, horizontal streaks stretching from the edges of the subject in the final image.
